seasonal: From a seasonal perspective, April is considered a very positive month for the S&P 500. Three out of four decade patterns point upward, and even if two of them are slightly positive, the overall bias is bullish. April is not a regular month - when the market starts to lose shape, this is exactly where the real value of the approach lies.

statistics.description: Over the last 30 years, approximately 68% probability of decline around April 2
Over the last 30 years, approximately 68% probability of decline around April 10
Over the last 30 years, the index closed higher on April 28 than on April 15 in ~83% of cases (25 out of 30)
Over the last 30 years, the index closed higher on April 28 than on April 15 in ~83% of cases (range statistic)

insights: April 2026 continues to be a very positive month for the S&P 500 from a seasonal perspective, with three out of four decade patterns pointing upward

Historical cycle analysis: 90-year cycle points to 1936 (Nazi Germany remilitarization), and other significant geopolitical parallels. The Iran attack materialized as forecasted in the annual and February reports

CNN Fear & Greed Index dropped to 19 points (Extreme Fear) by end of March - down from 57 at end of January and 43 at end of February. The index works as a contrarian indicator: extreme fear can signal buying opportunities, not selling
The S&P 500 decline from the highs reached 7.5-8.5% - this is still within the range of a normal correction, not a crash. A correction of 10-15% does not negate the positive primary trend
When risk starts to accumulate and take shape, the report is not an apocalyptic prediction - no fear, no drama. This is all part of the cycle, and sometimes what follows is an opportunity
The weekly chart shows an AB=CD pattern with potential completion around the 100-week moving average area near 6,175 and Fibonacci 38.2% retracement zone around 6,147-6,095
Daily chart weakness is where it starts to show first - the daily graph absorbs news and sentiment changes before weekly or monthly charts
When the market does not react to clearly bad news, it often means something worse is coming. But also: when sentiment reaches extreme fear levels, it can be a contrarian signal
2026 was described in the annual report as a psychologically very sensitive year for markets, and this is starting to materialize
Entry timing is not about catching the exact bottom - it is about waiting for confirmation after a turning point date, not jumping ahead of it
The discord community is recommended for real-time updates throughout the month as things develop

support.description: 20-week moving average area, strong weekly support, last strong support before deeper correction
Clear break below signals transition from shallow to deep correction
Major correction target - Fibonacci 38.2% retracement of rally from April 2025, overlaps with 100-week moving average
Weekly retest zone from previous highs, potential AB=CD pattern completion area
Fibonacci level, break below triggers deeper quality decline

resistance.description: Resistance zone near all-time highs on daily chart

technical.indicators: Weekly chart shows AB=CD pattern potentially completing near 6,175 area. 100-week moving average and Fibonacci 38.2% coincide at this zone. Daily chart shows clear weakness with fading buyer strength. D9 indicator previously lit extreme reading (8-9 range). CNN Fear & Greed at 19 (Extreme Fear). MACD remained below action line throughout last rally wave. 100-day and 200-day moving averages are important on the daily chart. The weekly cycle alignment in the first and third weeks of April is notable.

summary: April 2026 continues to be a very positive month seasonally for the S&P 500, with three out of four decade patterns pointing upward. However, the current correction (7.5-8.5% from highs) combined with extreme fear sentiment (CNN Fear & Greed at 19) creates a complex environment. Key turning dates are April 2, 6-9, 17, and 27-28. The important statistic shows that in 83% of the last 30 years, the index closed higher on April 28 than on April 15, suggesting the second half of April tends to be stronger. The 6,175 area (Fibonacci 38.2% + 100-week MA) is the major downside target if the correction deepens.
previous_month_review: March 2026 had four turning point dates. Date 1 (March 2): Worked. Initially appeared as if the price stalled and was stuck, but ultimately it marked the beginning of the next cycle downward. Date 2 (March 12): Worked, with a one-day offset. Marked the 'magnet' (50% of the movement) and from there the decline continued to the next turning date. Date 3 (March 18): Worked. Marked the high of the movement from the previous cycle to the next turning point. Date 4 (March 27): Worked. Even though the low was received a few trading days earlier, from around this area the upward movement began toward the next turning date in early April.
risk_notes: Maximum risk per trade should not exceed 1% of account on futures contracts
In times of futures, there is no place for interpretation - only numbers and execution
When the market begins to break down, the rules become more important than the data itself
Do not hold open positions over weekends without understanding the risk - one event outside trading hours can open the week with an aggressive gap
A correction of 10-15% does not negate a positive primary trend - it restores balance and reduces accumulated risk
Also take care of yourselves and your mental health - in months like these, not just the account is at risk
The information is for educational and learning purposes only - not investment advice
It is very important to be connected to the Discord community for real-time updates throughout the month
